name: Apple HIG Design Quality
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Apple Human Interface Guidelines — Deep Design Quality

This app should exemplify Apple's Human Interface Guidelines. Follow every rule below carefully.

COLOR SYSTEM:
- Build a small Color extension (e.g. Theme.swift) with 2-4 named colors: primary, secondary, accent, destructive. Use Color("AccentColor") or define via hex extension. Reference these throughout rather than scattering raw hex.
- Always use semantic system colors for backgrounds: Color(.systemBackground), Color(.secondarySystemBackground), Color(.systemGroupedBackground). These adapt to dark mode automatically.
- For text: use Color.primary (auto-adapts), Color.secondary for metadata, Color(.tertiaryLabel) for hints. Never hardcode Color.white or Color.black for text.
- Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text, 3:1 for large titles. If your accent color is light, darken it on light backgrounds.

TYPOGRAPHY HIERARCHY (strict):
- Hero numbers / primary values: .largeTitle or .system(size: 48, weight: .bold, design: .rounded)
- Screen titles: handled by .navigationTitle — don't duplicate.
- Section headers: .font(.headline) or .font(.title3.weight(.semibold)).
- Body content: .font(.body). Lists, paragraphs, descriptions.
- Metadata / timestamps / secondary info: .font(.caption) or .font(.footnote), with .foregroundStyle(.secondary).
- Numeric displays (timers, counters, money): use .monospacedDigit() to prevent layout jitter.
- NEVER use .body for everything. Establish at least 3 levels of visual hierarchy on every screen.

SPACING & LAYOUT:
- Use 16pt horizontal padding (minimum) on all screens. 20pt is better for content-heavy screens.
- VStack spacing: 8pt for tight groups, 12pt for related items, 20pt for section separation.
- Card internal padding: 16pt all sides. Corner radius: 12-16pt. Shadow: .shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.08), radius: 8, y: 4).
- Touch targets: 44×44pt minimum for all interactive elements. Use .frame(minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44) on small buttons.
- Use .padding(.vertical, 12) on list rows and form fields for comfortable tapping.
- Always use ScrollView if content might overflow. Add .safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom) for floating action buttons.

NAVIGATION PATTERNS:
- Flat apps (3-5 sections): TabView at root, each tab gets its own NavigationStack.
- Hierarchical apps: NavigationStack with NavigationLink(value:) and .navigationDestination(for:). Show .navigationTitle and .toolbar.
- Modal tasks (creation, editing, settings): .sheet(item:) with a toolbar containing Cancel (leading) and Done/Save (trailing, .borderedProminent).
- Full-screen takeover (camera, media, immersive content): .fullScreenCover.
- Search: .searchable(text:prompt:) on NavigationStack or List. System handles animation and placement.
- Do NOT put NavigationStack inside another NavigationStack. Do NOT put TabView inside a sheet.

BUTTON HIERARCHY:
- Primary CTA (1 per screen max): Button { } label: { }.buttonStyle(.borderedProminent).controlSize(.large).
- Secondary: .buttonStyle(.bordered).
- Tertiary / text links: .buttonStyle(.plain) with accent foregroundStyle.
- Destructive: Button(role: .destructive) — gets red automatically.
- Icon-only buttons: always add .accessibilityLabel("Description").
- Disabled state: .disabled(condition) — system handles dimming.

FORMS & INPUT:
- Settings-type screens: Form { Section("Header") { } } — gives grouped inset styling automatically.
- Label + TextField: in Form, use LabeledContent or let Form auto-label. Standalone: put a Text label above.
- Set .textContentType(.emailAddress), .keyboardType(.emailAddress), .autocorrectionDisabled() as appropriate.
- SecureField for passwords. Toggle for booleans. Picker for enums.
- Validation: show inline red Text below the field, not a blocking alert. Use .foregroundStyle(.red) + .font(.caption).

LISTS:
- Settings / grouped data: .listStyle(.insetGrouped).
- Content feeds / timelines: .listStyle(.plain).
- Swipe-to-delete: .swipeActions(edge: .trailing) { Button(role: .destructive) { ... } }.
- Pull-to-refresh: .refreshable { await reload() }.
- Empty state: if items.isEmpty { ContentUnavailableView("No Items", systemImage: "tray", description: Text("Add items to get started.")) }. ContentUnavailableView is iOS 17+.

ALERTS & CONFIRMATIONS:
- Destructive confirmation: .alert("Delete Item?", isPresented:) with a .destructive button labeled with the action ("Delete", not "OK").
- Multi-option: .confirmationDialog("Title", isPresented:) with role-labeled buttons. Always include cancel.
- Success feedback: use inline animation (checkmark appear, color flash), NOT an alert.
- Errors: show inline banner or .alert with clear recovery steps.

ACCESSIBILITY (mandatory):
- Every Image(systemName:) button needs .accessibilityLabel("Descriptive action").
- Decorative images: .accessibilityHidden(true).
- Group related views: .accessibilityElement(children: .combine).
- Test: every screen must be navigable by VoiceOver in logical order.
- Dynamic Type: all text uses semantic styles (.body, .title2, etc.) — never hardcoded .system(size:) for body content.
- Reduce Motion: wrap spring animations in if !UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled or use .animation(.default, value:) which respects it.

ANIMATION:
- Default: .spring(response: 0.35, dampingFraction: 0.85). Natural, not bouncy.
- Quick state changes (toggle, select): .animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.2), value: state).
- List insertions/deletions: withAnimation(.spring(response: 0.4, dampingFraction: 0.8)) { items.append(new) }.
- Page transitions: NavigationStack handles these; don't add custom transitions to NavigationLink.
- Loading shimmer: use .redacted(reason: .placeholder) with a shimmer overlay for skeleton loading.
- NEVER use .linear animation — it feels robotic. NEVER animate with duration > 0.5s for UI state changes.

DARK MODE:
- Must work in both light and dark. Test by wrapping previews in .preferredColorScheme(.dark).
- All backgrounds: use semantic colors (Color(.systemBackground)) or materials (.regularMaterial). These auto-adapt.
- Shadows: reduce opacity in dark mode — .shadow(color: .black.opacity(colorScheme == .dark ? 0.3 : 0.08), ...).
- Dividers: use Divider() (auto-adapts) or Color(.separator).

SF SYMBOLS:
- Use SF Symbols (Image(systemName:)) for all icons. They scale with Dynamic Type automatically.
- Rendering modes: .symbolRenderingMode(.hierarchical) for depth on detail icons, .monochrome for toolbars, .multicolor for system indicators (battery, wifi).
- Sizing: match adjacent text with .font(.body) or use .imageScale(.medium/.large). Don't use fixed .frame() on symbols.
- Filled vs outline: filled variants (.fill suffix) for selected/active tab items; outline for inactive.

EMPTY & LOADING STATES:
- Empty list: ContentUnavailableView with SF Symbol + description + optional action button.
- Loading: ProgressView() centered, or skeleton loading with .redacted(reason: .placeholder).
- Error: ContentUnavailableView with exclamationmark.triangle + error message + retry button.
- Never show a blank white/black screen. Every state must have intentional UI.
antiPatterns: Using Color.white/Color.black for backgrounds — breaks dark mode; use Color(.systemBackground)
Hardcoding .system(size: 16) for body text — use .font(.body) for Dynamic Type support
Missing .accessibilityLabel on icon-only buttons — VoiceOver can't describe the action
Using .linear animation — feels robotic; use .spring() or .easeInOut
Showing success via .alert — use inline animation/feedback instead
Empty screen with no placeholder — always show ContentUnavailableView or loading state
Multiple .borderedProminent buttons on one screen — only one primary CTA per screen
Nesting NavigationStack inside NavigationStack — causes double navigation bars and broken back buttons
